macro_segments.title: Review of Vestos (Regular Period) Status: Biblical vs. Rabbinic and the Three Positions

micro_segments.title: Summary of Previous Day's Debate on Vestos de'Oraisa vs. Vestos de'Rabbanan
Three Possibilities for Woman's Status After Expected Period: Completely Tahora, Completely Temeia, or Safek
Rabbi Yochanan's Position: Woman is Safek Temeia After Period, Can Assume Tahora After Mikvah Time
Rishonim Debate on Rabbi Yochanan: Tosafot vs. Rashba on Meaning of Vestos de'Rabbanan
Concern About Husband Assuming Wife's Status Without Asking: Power Dynamics and Silence
Difference Between Woman Without Vestos vs. Woman With Established Vestos

macro_segments.title: Principle of Ein Safek Motzi Midei Vadai: When Doubt Can Override Certainty

micro_segments.title: Statement of Principle: Ein Safek Motzi Midei Vadai - Doubt Cannot Displace Certainty
Baraita of Servant of Masik: Miscarriage in Pit, Kohen Observes, Chachamim Rule Kohen Tahor
Gemara's Reading: Original Problem Was Not Vadai but Rather Safek Whether Fetus or Discharge
Alternative Reading: Weasels and Moles Present, So Definitely Dragged Away
Conclusion: Safek Can Override Vadai When Likelihood Is So High It Functions as Vadai
Distinction Between Chazaka as Status Quo vs. Chazaka as Established Pattern of Behavior

macro_segments.title: Vestos de'Oraisa vs. Vestos de'Rabbanan: Direct Debate and Inconclusive Baraita

micro_segments.title: Question Posed to Rav Nachman: Is Vestos de'Oraisa or de'Rabbanan?
Baraita: Woman with Vestos Did Not Check During Period, Later Saw Bleeding
Huna in Name of Rav: Must Be Concerned for Both Day of Vestos and Day of Seeing
Gemara: Attempt to Prove Vestos de'Oraisa from Baraita Fails—Proof Is Not Conclusive
Gemara's Reading: Key Evidence Is That Woman Saw, Not That Period Passed

macro_segments.title: Rav vs. Shmuel: Checking After Vestos Period and Proof of Taharah

micro_segments.title: Rav's Position: If Woman Checks After Vestos and Finds Blood, She's Temeia; If Tahora, She's Tahora
Shmuel's Position: Even With Check Finding Tahora, She's Temeia—Oreiach Bizmano Ba
Analysis: Debate Concerns Whether Check Immediately After Vestos Counts as Proof
Explanation of Bedikah Timing: Check During Vestos Period vs. Check After Period Ends
Gemara's Resolution: Agreement on Vestos de'Oraisa, Difference on When Bedikah Proves Taharah

macro_segments.title: Tannaim Debate on Vestos: Rabbi Eliezer (Definite Temeia) vs. Rabbi Yehoshua (Check Needed)

micro_segments.title: Baraita: Rabbi Eliezer Says Niddah; Rabbi Yehoshua Says Check
Parallel Tannaim Debate: Rabbi Meir (Definite Temeia) vs. Chachamim (Safek)
Proof from Mishnah: Rabbi Meir on Woman in Cave—Fear Stops Bleeding, Suggesting Vestos de'Oraisa
Interpretation: If Fear Prevents Bleeding, Then Without Fear She's Temeia—Suggests Oraisa

macro_segments.title: Woman Bleeding from Wound vs. Niddah Blood: Status of Uterine Source and Vestos

micro_segments.title: Baraita: Woman with Wound Bleeding During Niddah Period—Rabban Gamliel Rules Tahora
Problem: How Can She Be Tahora After Vestos Passes Without Check?
Ramban's Answer: Waiving Rabbinic Check Requirement When Bedikah Impossible
Gemara: Debate Whether to Read as Vestos de'Oraisa or de'Rabbanan
Ravina's Reinterpretation: All Agree Vestos de'Rabbanan; Debate Is About Mekor Status
Mekor (Uterus) as Source: Whether Non-Menstrual Blood from Uterus Is Tamei
Rabbi Shim'on ben Elazar: Woman Tahora but Blood Tamei from Mekor
Rabbi's Counter: If Not Concerned for Vestos de'Rabbanan, Also Not Concerned for Mekor Taharah
Tosafot's Concern: Limits on Toleh b'Makka—Must Have Supporting Evidence Blood Is Not Niddah

macro_segments.title: Practical Halakha: Vestos de'Rabbanan as Accepted Psak and Default Thirty-Day Cycle

micro_segments.title: Conclusion: All Authorities Pasken Vestos de'Rabbanan Based on Gemara's Reinterpretation
Halakha Lema'aseh: Woman with Vestos Must Check After Period; Later Check Suffices
Rashi's Innovation: Thirty-Day Cycle as Default Vestos for Woman Without Regular Period

macro_segments.title: Mishnah: Beit Shammai vs. Beit Hillel on Checking After Each Act of Sex

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Beit Shammai Requires Two Cloths Per Act or Checking by Candlelight
Mishnah: Beit Hillel Says Two Cloths Sufficient for Entire Night
Tosafot vs. Rashi: Two Cloths Interpretation—Husband/Wife Cloths vs. Before/After Cloths
Debate on Frequency: Tosafot—Check Each Time; Rashi—Check Beginning and End of Night

macro_segments.title: Baraita Debate: Beit Shammai vs. Beit Hillel on Masking Blood Evidence

micro_segments.title: Concern: Same Cloth Masks Earlier Blood Evidence; Semen Covers Previous Stains
Beit Hillel's Response: All Checking Has Limitations; Never Get Perfect Proof
Beit Shammai's Counter: Multiple Maskings Worse Than Single Masking

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehoshua's Stringency and Talmidim's Complaint: Lengthening Demands in This World

micro_segments.title: Baraita: Rabbi Yehoshua Favors Beit Shammai; Students Complain of Burden
Rabbi Yehoshua's Response: Better to Burden in This World for Life in World to Come

macro_segments.title: From Taharot Concerns to Practical Prohibition: Checking Lost Cloth and Sexual Prohibition

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Zeira: From All Positions Learn That Ba'al Nefesh Should Not Have Sex Twice Same Night
Rava's Qualification: All Checking Rules Only Apply in World of Taharot, Not for Practical Issur
Status Quo: Can Assume Tahora If Left in That Status; Cannot If Left in Temeia Status
Lost Cloth Case: If Cloth Checked Then Lost, Cannot Have Sex Until New Check
Rabbi Ila's Challenge: But Other Cases Allow Sex Without Checking Cloth Immediately
Rava's Distinction: With Evidence Available Later vs. No Evidence Available; Low-Level Concern vs. High

macro_segments.title: Prohibition on Daytime Sexual Relations: Conception, Privacy, and Intimacy

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan: Man Forbidden to Have Sex with Wife During Daytime
Rav Huna's Source: Conception Associated with Night; Day Not Conducive to Conception
Resh Lakish: One Who Vulgarizes His Ways Should Die; Privacy and Respect Required
Rashi's Reading: Not About Sex Being Dirty, But About Seeing Something Repulsive in Bright Light
Alternative Understanding: Mystery, Intimacy, and Connection Over Physical Exposure

topical_tags.term: Ein Safek Motzi Midei Vadai (Doubt Cannot Displace Certainty)
Oreiach Bizmano Ba (Guest Comes at Appointed Time)
